channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel's stance on Lebanon ceasefire complicates U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: The U.S.-Iran agreement hinges on Israel accepting a ceasefire in Lebanon, something it is reluctant to do.
item.content:encoded: The U.S.-Iran agreement hinges on Israel accepting a ceasefire in Lebanon, something it is reluctant to do.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Israeli ambassador to U.S. says Israel is 'not going to withdraw from South Lebanon'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Israel's ambassador to the U.S. Michael Leiter about the peace deal the Trump administration says it's made with Iran.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel's reluctance on Lebanon ceasefire complicates U.S.-Iran deal, first full day of G7 summit gets underway in France, what to watch as voters in several states head to the polls for primaries.
item.content:encoded: Israel's reluctance on Lebanon ceasefire complicates U.S.-Iran deal, first full day of G7 summit gets underway in France, what to watch as voters in several states head to the polls for primaries.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Faith's role in U.S. politics 'requires humility,' not certainty, says Sen. Warnock
item.description: Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Senator Raphael Warnock of Georgia about his new book, "The Crooked Places Made Straight: Reflections on the Moral Meaning of America."
item.content:encoded:
Michel Martin speaks with Democratic Senator Raphael Warnock of Georgia about his new book, "The Crooked Places Made Straight: Reflections on the Moral Meaning of America."

(Image credit: Heather Diehl)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Trump administration's immigration crackdown gives local jails a financial boost
item.description: The Trump administration's crackdown on immigrants in the country illegally is helping provide local jails with much needed revenue. But some people want local officials to stop.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration's crackdown on immigrants in the country illegally is helping provide local jails with much needed revenue. But some people want local officials to stop.

item.title: Visa and OpenAI integrate Visa's secure global payment directly into ChatGPT
item.description: Would you hand an AI chatbot your credit card? This week, Visa's deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI became the latest step in the march toward a future where AI offers to shop on your behalf.
item.content:encoded: Would you hand an AI chatbot your credit card? This week, Visa's deal with ChatGPT maker OpenAI became the latest step in the march toward a future where AI offers to shop on your behalf.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Lower water levels put Grand Canyon river recreation at risk
item.description: The new climate-changed reality of less water in the Colorado River has Grand Canyon river guides worried about the future viability of their businesses.
item.content:encoded: The new climate-changed reality of less water in the Colorado River has Grand Canyon river guides worried about the future viability of their businesses.
item.dc:creator: Chris Clements

item.title: Fears mount aid cuts could lead to return of HIV/AIDS epidemic's child-led households
item.description: HIV/AIDS medications are harder to get due to aid cuts, raising concerns of a return to child-headed households that were prevalent before the U.S. tackled the HIV/AIDS epidemic.
item.content:encoded: HIV/AIDS medications are harder to get due to aid cuts, raising concerns of a return to child-headed households that were prevalent before the U.S. tackled the HIV/AIDS epidemic.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Book on deadly 2013 riots blocked from being sold in the country it happened in
item.description: Renowned comics journalist Joe Sacco's new book, "The Once and Future Riot," details the deadly 2013 riots in India, but it won't be sold in that country because the publisher blocked it.
item.content:encoded: Renowned comics journalist Joe Sacco's new book, "The Once and Future Riot," details the deadly 2013 riots in India, but it won't be sold in that country because the publisher blocked it.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: G7 leaders meet for first full day of summit Tuesday
item.description: President Trump meets with G7 leaders, as well as Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and the heads of Qatar and UAE, during the key summit's first full day.
item.content:encoded: President Trump meets with G7 leaders, as well as Ukrainian President Zelenskyy and the heads of Qatar and UAE, during the key summit's first full day.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben
